meta.description: Role-based security pathways, portfolio milestones, and weekly planning templates.

roleProfiles.label: Cloud Security Engineer
targetArtifacts: Cloud IR playbook with runbook automation

roleProfiles.label: Application Security Engineer
targetArtifacts: API security test suite and CI checks

roleProfiles.label: Detection Engineer / SOC
targetArtifacts: Sigma rule pack with ATT&CK mapping

roleProfiles.label: Red Team / Offensive Security
targetArtifacts: Privilege escalation and lateral movement lab notes

roleProfiles.label: ML and AI Security Engineer
targetArtifacts: AI threat model and control mapping

phases.name: Foundation and Baseline
objectives: Solidify Linux, networking, IAM, and logging fundamentals
Establish repeatable cloud lab architecture
Publish first cloud threat-model artifact
projectMilestones: Cloud attack-surface map and hardening baseline

phases.name: Cloud Security Specialization
objectives: Master identity security, key management, and telemetry
Implement policy-as-code and guardrail automation
Design cloud incident response workflows

phases.name: Architecture and Leadership
objectives: Advance to platform and architecture depth
Translate technical controls into business risk language
Build recruiter-ready technical case studies
projectMilestones: Cloud security architecture case study with measurable outcomes

learningPaths.name: Practical AppSec and Bug Bounty Track (9 months)

phases.name: Web Security Core
objectives: Complete SQLi/XSS/IDOR/SSRF core labs
Build methodology in Burp + scripts
Publish high-quality writeups
projectMilestones: OWASP Top 10 testing checklist and demos

phases.name: Advanced App and API Security
objectives: Practice business-logic and API abuse patterns
Integrate security checks into CI pipelines
Document secure coding remediations

phases.name: Offensive Depth and Hiring Readiness
objectives: Develop advanced exploitation and reporting rigor
Create interview narratives from practical work
Package portfolio for target market and role
projectMilestones: End-to-end assessment report with remediation priorities

learningPaths.name: Detection Engineering and IR Track (10 months)

phases.name: Analyst Fundamentals
objectives: Build strong log-analysis and triage workflow
Understand ATT&CK mapping and detection coverage
Tune false-positive heavy detections

phases.name: Detection Engineering
objectives: Write and validate detection logic at scale
Automate enrichment and triage pipelines
Build incident readiness playbooks
projectMilestones: Rule quality dashboard and validation framework

phases.name: IR and Forensics Depth
objectives: Perform structured incident investigations
Capture forensic evidence and timeline narratives
Improve mean-time-to-detect and contain metrics

learningPaths.name: GRC and Security Management Track (9 months)

phases.name: Governance Baseline
objectives: Map controls frameworks to evidence requirements
Build risk taxonomy and asset inventory
Create compliance reporting templates

phases.name: Risk and Assurance
objectives: Quantify risk using repeatable scoring models
Build internal audit and evidence collection cadence
Align security outcomes to business objectives

phases.name: Leadership Readiness
objectives: Develop executive communication discipline
Create strategic security roadmap
Build board-facing KPI and KRI dashboard

weeklyBriefTemplates.label: Balanced Deep Work Week
dayPlan: Day 1: Scope goals and threat assumptions
Day 2: Deep lab session with evidence capture
Day 3: Build or automate one control
Day 4: Practice challenge and validate outcomes
Day 5: Writeup and remediation narrative
Day 6: KPI review and backlog triage
Day 7: Recovery + lightweight revision

weeklyBriefTemplates.label: Interview Sprint Week
dayPlan: Day 1: Role-specific concept refresh
Day 2: Time-boxed technical scenario
Day 3: System design and tradeoff articulation
Day 4: Security incident reasoning drill
Day 5: STAR stories with measurable impact
Day 6: Mock interview and gap scoring
Day 7: Close top three readiness gaps

weeklyBriefTemplates.label: Certification Exam Push Week
dayPlan: Day 1: Domain coverage planning and weak-area mapping
Day 2: Focused domain revision and flash recall
Day 3: Scenario-based question blocks
Day 4: Lab-backed concept reinforcement
Day 5: Full practice test and postmortem
Day 6: Revision on failure clusters
Day 7: Final confidence pass and exam logistics

mlsec: Prompt injection and guardrail test harnesses
offensive: TryHackMe and Hack The Box paths
